spoken_briefing:
Today in AI tools for specialty contractors and small general contractors.

Starting with bidding and estimating. STACK Construction Technologies named a new CEO, Viyas Sundaram, in March 2026 to lead its next chapter of AI innovation, with the platform crossing thirty-one point eight million dollars in annual revenue. STACK remains a leading cloud-based estimating platform for trade subcontractors, moving teams from uploaded plans to bid-ready numbers fast. Pricing starts at two thousand nine hundred ninety-nine dollars per year, and it connects with Procore for budget and cost code synchronization.

Togal dot AI offers three pricing tiers for 2026: Essential at one hundred ninety-nine dollars per user per month, Growth at two hundred ninety-nine dollars per user per month billed annually, and a Business tier on custom quote for teams of four or more. Its AI-driven floor plan takeoffs claim ninety-eight percent accuracy, with estimators completing full architectural takeoffs in twelve minutes instead of hours. The platform has now analyzed more than three million construction plans. Coastal Construction documented saving fourteen and a half hours per plan set, adding up to roughly one million dollars in annual cost savings. NC Painting used it to scale to sixty bids per month in under sixty days.

BidIntell is a pre-bid decision platform built specifically for specialty subcontractors who need to filter which invitations are worth pursuing. It scores incoming bid requests on a one-hundred-point BidIndex based on location fit, contract terms, your relationship history with the GC, and trade match. Pricing starts at forty-nine dollars per user per month, making it one of the most accessible AI tools on the market right now.

Beam AI from Attentive dot AI raised thirty point five million dollars in a Series B round in November 2025, bringing total funding to forty-eight million dollars and validating the platform's momentum with more than twelve hundred contractors and suppliers on board. The platform combines automated quantity takeoffs with human review to ensure accuracy across HVAC, electrical, plumbing, mechanical, and civil trades, and is now expanding beyond takeoffs into the full preconstruction lifecycle including estimating and bidding workflows. Pricing is custom-quoted based on volume.

Downtobid is an AI-powered bid management platform that reads construction plans, identifies every trade scope automatically, and sends personalized bid invitations to verified local subcontractors from a database of more than two hundred fifty thousand commercial subs. General contractors start at one hundred forty-nine dollars per month, and subcontractors accessing the full platform pay two hundred ninety-nine dollars per month. Personalized invites using the platform generate two times more responses than generic portal emails.

ConstructConnect Takeoff Boost is an AI-powered takeoff service built on Google Cloud using Gemini and NVIDIA computer vision. It automatically classifies, detects, counts, and measures areas, linears, and objects directly from digital plan sets and converts them into structured measurements in seconds. Available now to ConstructConnect customers through both OnScreen Takeoff and PlanSwift with Takeoff Boost, which launched May sixth, twenty twenty-six. Pricing is custom-quoted.

Drawer AI is a purpose-built electrical takeoff and estimating platform for electrical subcontractors and estimators. It reads PDF blueprints to detect and quantify all electrical devices, fixtures, and panels, generates branch circuit routing with automatic wire sizing, and merges multiple drawing files into a single layout. Backed by five million dollars in seed funding from Brick and Mortar Ventures and Base-Ten Partners. Starr Electric Company analyzed twenty-six hundred fixtures and thirty-four hundred power devices with a seventy percent time savings using the platform. Pricing starts at one hundred fifty dollars per user per month.

TaksoAi is an AI takeoff platform purpose-built for mechanical, HVAC, piping, and plumbing subcontractors. Founded in Toronto in 2021, it runs a proprietary AI engine that identifies sixty or more pipe and HVAC fitting types, processing most mechanical plans in under fifteen minutes. Its SpecTool feature automates specification analysis so estimators can instantly query parameters like pressure classes across an entire spec document. TaksoAi has also announced a strategic integration with Wendes Systems, embedding its AI takeoff directly into Wendes HVAC and mechanical estimating workflows. Pricing is custom-quoted.

XBuild is an AI-native agentic estimating platform founded by former Uber and Postmates executives. Currently live for roofing contractors, it ingests measurement reports from EagleView, GAF QuickScope, Hover, and Roofr plus job photos and produces an editable estimate with Good, Better, Best pricing in under fifteen minutes. The platform raised nineteen million dollars in a Series A led by N47 with Andreessen Horowitz in January 2026, with more than fifteen thousand projects processed in year one representing over two hundred fifty million dollars in construction volume. Expansion to additional commercial trades including concrete, painting, glazing, HVAC, and plumbing is on the roadmap.

Trimble LiveCount AI Automate Takeoff automatically detects and counts electrical and MEP devices from PDF drawings within Trimble's Accubid platform, achieving a ninety-five percent drawing scale auto-detection rate and saving more than thirty-five hundred estimating hours annually across the user base. It has identified, classified, and counted more than two hundred thousand objects per month. The feature is included in existing Trimble Accubid subscriptions at no additional cost.

Quotr dot AI from Quotr, backed by Berkeley SkyDeck, uploads PDF plans or Revit model data to auto-detect rooms, counts, and openings with estimator control, applying built-in item libraries or custom labor and assembly rules with location-based pricing. It claims ninety percent faster takeoffs and ninety-five percent accuracy, and includes procurement workflows alongside estimating. Monthly platform plans are available with a seven-day free trial.

Space AI is a newer AI-powered construction bidding and project management platform founded in 2025. It combines bid tracking, AI competitiveness scoring, scope gap analysis, and award management in one interface. There is a free tier for basic bidding plus a Professional plan at ninety-nine dollars per user per month.

ConstructionBids dot AI is an AI-powered government and public construction bid aggregation platform that consolidates live bid opportunities from more than two thousand federal, state, and local procurement portals into a single AI-scored dashboard. It delivers fit scores matched to company trade and geography, automated deadline alerts, scope matching, and AI risk analysis on contract terms. Pricing starts at forty-nine dollars per month.

Kreo from Kreo Software is an AI-assisted two-dimensional takeoff and three-dimensional BIM cost estimating platform that automatically detects elements, cross-references data across drawing sheets, and generates cost reports. It supports both PDF takeoffs and BIM model cost estimation with multi-user cloud collaboration. Pricing starts at thirty-five dollars per user per month, making it one of the most accessible AI estimating platforms currently available.

Fresco is an AI takeoff and estimating platform purpose-built exclusively for Division Eight specialty contractors covering doors, frames, and hardware. It automates the complete takeoff by reading project specifications, cross-referencing hardware sets against door schedules, and producing a quantity takeoff with ninety-nine percent claimed accuracy. The platform has completed more than three thousand takeoffs and is backed by Y Combinator. Pricing is by contact.

Rudus is an AI takeoff and estimation platform built exclusively for concrete subcontractors. It auto-classifies every sheet type and uses computer vision to detect every concrete structure across the full drawing set, building a line-item estimate with more than three hundred items. It reduces concrete takeoff from twenty to forty hours per bid to under six hours and is backed by Y Combinator. Export goes directly to HCSS HeavyBid, Sage Estimating, B2W, or Excel.

Bidi Contracting is an AI-powered bid management platform for general contractors that unifies AI plan takeoff, subcontractor solicitation, and bid leveling in one workflow. Its AI trains on actual sub bid history to generate estimates reflecting what specific local subs charge rather than national averages. Pricing starts at approximately thirty-six hundred dollars per year for GC bid management.

Moving to submittal and RFI tracking. Trunk Tools expanded its Autodesk Forma integration in May 2026, adding a full suite of agents including bid analysis, SOP adherence, and scope writing on top of its existing RFI management and submittal review capabilities, all available directly to project teams running Autodesk Forma Build. Its three core products remain TrunkSubmittal for spec-matched submittal review, TrunkText for conversational queries across project documents, and TrunkReview for analyzing changes in newly issued drawing sets. Cleveland Construction reported saving more than seven hundred ninety hours and sixty thousand dollars across four projects. Pricing is by custom quote.

Procore's AI platform, Procore Helix, continues expanding in 2026 following its Datagrid acquisition in January 2026. Procore launched five native AI agents on June first, twenty twenty-six: a Submittals agent, an RFI agent, a Deep Search agent, a Daily Log agent, and a Contract Review agent, and relaunched its Common Data Environment with connected data and agentic AI at its core. In March 2026, Procore announced a partnership with NVIDIA to integrate the Procore platform with the NVIDIA Omniverse DSX Blueprint for digital twin capabilities on AI factory and data center construction projects. Procore is priced on annual construction volume.

Civils dot AI extracts data directly from drawings, specs, and building codes to automate RFI generation and compliance tracking. It is built for both GC and specialty sub teams, and pricing is by custom quote.

BuildSync is a purpose-built AI submittal review platform that compares submitted product data against project specifications with ninety-five percent accuracy. It cuts first-submission rejection rates from thirty-five percent to five percent and reduces review time by seventy to eighty percent. BuildSync integrates directly with Procore and Autodesk Construction Cloud. Pricing is custom-quoted.

SubmittalLink is a platform purpose-built for subcontractors managing construction submittals and RFIs on an accessible subscription model. It converts spec books into structured submittal logs automatically, sends notification-driven routing across the team, and uses AI to parse drawings, generate status reports, and automate coversheet creation. All plans include unlimited users, projects, and file storage. Pricing starts at one hundred fifty dollars per month for teams with under five million dollars in annual construction volume, and two hundred fifty dollars per month for five to twenty-five million dollar teams.

Constructable is an AI-native field and project management platform built for mid-market general contractors running twenty million to one hundred fifty million dollar commercial projects. Constructable pins RFIs directly to the drawing location where questions arise, uses an AI Answer Engine to pull answers from past project data instantly, and includes unlimited users on all plans with no per-seat fees and no overage charges. The platform covers drawings, RFIs, submittals, punch lists, and financial management in one system and onboards teams in under twenty-one days. Pricing starts at eight hundred fifty dollars per month for field management, two thousand dollars per month for field and project management, and three thousand five hundred dollars per month for the complete field, project, and financial management tier.

Nomic AI is an AEC-specialized AI platform built on large language models trained on construction documentation including drawing sets up to two hundred pages and specifications up to eight hundred pages. It automates submittal review cross-referencing product data against drawings and specs, and checks building code compliance against three hundred eighty or more building codes. Pricing is forty dollars per user per month with a minimum twenty-five seat commitment.

Now for field management and daily reporting. CompanyCam is a photo-first field documentation platform that achieved unicorn status after closing a Series C funding round in August 2025. Its AI Walkthrough Note feature lets a superintendent walk the site, speak observations, and snap photos, and AI automatically assembles a structured daily report. CompanyCam acquired Beam Finance in March 2026 to add AI estimating, payments, and invoicing to the platform. Plans start at ninety-nine dollars per month with a three-user minimum.

eSUB is the subcontractor-exclusive field management platform that has added meaningful AI capabilities in 2026, including automated daily report drafting from field supervisor voice notes, predictive labor cost analysis that flags tasks trending over budget, and intelligent RFI drafting that suggests relevant specification sections. A mechanical sub using the AI daily report drafting feature reduced foreman paperwork from forty-five minutes to ten minutes per day. Pricing is by custom quote.

Buildots has added major new construction firms to its customer roster in 2026, now including STO Building Group, Mortenson, Ledcor, and Pomerleau alongside existing clients Turner Construction, JE Dunn, Digital Realty, and Intel. The platform uses helmet-mounted cameras and computer vision to capture real-time site progress against the schedule. Total funding reached one hundred sixty-six million dollars on a forty-five million dollar Series D. Pricing is custom-quoted by project size.

Raken received a majority stake investment from Sverica Capital in September 2025, giving the field reporting platform new private equity backing to accelerate product development and market expansion. Raken now serves more than forty-five hundred construction firms and seventy thousand users with daily reports, time tracking, toolbox talks, and production tracking. Pricing starts at fifteen dollars per user per month for the Lite plan and twenty-five dollars per user per month for Pro.

BuildOps launched its Projects module in May 2026, giving specialty contractors an AI-native system to run multi-month construction work from kickoff to closeout alongside their service operations. Powered by OpsAI for scheduling, document management, field coordination, and daily reporting. BuildOps raised one hundred twenty-seven million dollars in a Series C round, reaching a one billion dollar valuation. Pricing is by custom quote.

InspectMind AI is a Y Combinator-backed platform that generates field reports, punch lists, safety inspections, and site walkthroughs in minutes. Its Field Reports product captures photos and voice notes on mobile, works offline, and produces structured reports at one hundred dollars per user per month. Plan checking starts at one hundred dollars per review. It integrates with Procore and Autodesk Construction Cloud.

iFieldSmart from iFieldSmart Technologies is a construction management platform with reality capture and AI-powered field documentation. Its Lens three-sixty feature turns a single site walkthrough into a complete digital twin. Pricing starts at fourteen dollars and ninety-nine cents per user per month.

Kahua is a flexible construction management platform with built-in AI through its Noa assistant, which automates repetitive project workflows, uncovers insights from project data, and lets teams build custom apps in minutes. Pricing is custom-quoted with implementation costs typically ranging from five thousand to fifty thousand dollars or more.

OpenSpace is a Visual Intelligence Platform that uses three-sixty cameras to automatically map site photos to floor plans and BIM models. It launched OpenSpace Field in general availability in February 2026, an image-based field task and issue management solution that connects captured site reality directly to execution workflows. Suffolk Construction documented an eighty-six percent speed improvement in documenting issues using the platform's AI Autolocation and AI Voice Notes. Vinci Construction UK saved six thousand or more hours annually on documentation. Pricing is based on annual construction volume.

Novade Solutions is an AI-powered field management platform used by more than one hundred thousand users in twenty-five or more countries. Key AI features include speech-to-forms that convert field voice observations into structured mobile data, scan-to-form that photographs paper forms and converts them into digital templates, AI daily report generation, and a predictive safety monitoring engine trained on real-world incident data that achieves up to eighty-eight percent accuracy in hazard risk scoring. A free plan is available for up to five users and five projects.

Benetics AI is an AI voice assistant for construction field documentation. Field crews speak naturally into the mobile app to capture site conditions, deliveries, safety observations, and progress updates, and AI converts voice to professional structured reports in seconds without stopping work. Two hundred fifty or more companies are using the platform across the US and Canada with users reporting up to seventy to eighty percent reduction in admin time. Available on iOS and Android.

BuilderHelp from BuilderHelp launched its AI construction management platform on June first, twenty twenty-six, targeting general contractors in the five million to sixty-five million dollar range. The platform was developed by industry professionals and features predictive AI scheduling, centralized communication across subs and project teams, task tracking with real-time progress visibility, invoice and budget monitoring, and document management for plans, specs, permits, and contracts. More than fifty general contractors were on the waitlist at launch. Pricing is available directly through the company.

KYRO AI is a unified field management platform for construction, vegetation management, and storm restoration contractors. It combines project management, crew communication, time tracking, payroll, and daily reporting with AI voice translation across thirty or more languages and OCR document scanning. Pricing starts at twenty-one dollars per user per month billed annually.

Klutch AI is a multi-agent AI construction management platform offering specialized agents across the project lifecycle, including Archie AI for permit review, Bob AI for field documentation via SMS and WhatsApp without a portal login, Petra AI for estimating and bid leveling, and Hailey AI for warranty ticket management. The platform raised eight million dollars in a seed round led by Bain Capital Ventures. Pricing is by contact.

Opusense AI is a Y Combinator-backed field inspection report automation platform. Field inspectors walk a site, speak observations aloud, and snap photos while AI generates a detailed inspection report in real time on iOS and Android. Claims up to eighty percent reduction in inspection report production time.

Hardline is a voice-first construction documentation tool that captures jobsite phone calls in English and Spanish, transcribes them in real time, and uses AI to extract and structure action items into daily logs, RFIs, punch lists, and change orders automatically from the conversation itself. It raised two million dollars in a pre-seed round from Mucker Capital and is active across ten states and two countries.

Track3D is an AI Reality Intelligence platform that ingests three-sixty cameras, drones, laser scanners, and LiDAR data into a single platform, applies AI-driven progress tracking, quantifies work installed by trade, and generates automated progress reports. Named to BuiltWorlds forty AI-Driven AEC Solutions to Watch in 2026. Pricing is custom-quoted.

Doxel AI uses three-sixty cameras and computer vision to compare actual construction progress against BIM models in real time, automatically tracking work installed across all visible trades and more than eighty stages of construction. The platform generates color-coded cost versus schedule reports that give project teams early warning of slippage before it compounds. DPR Construction is among its notable customers. Doxel has raised fifty-six point five million dollars backed by Insight Partners and Andreessen Horowitz. It integrates with Procore, Autodesk Construction Cloud, and Oracle Primavera P six. Pricing is custom-quoted.

For time and materials and billing automation. HCSS HeavyJob is the go-to standard for heavy and civil specialty contractors doing time-and-material work. It pulls T and M billing data directly from field time cards and generates accurate invoices with minimal manual effort. The 2026 Spring Release added a new AI-powered audit log summarization tool and direct HeavyBid-to-HeavyJob estimate handoff. Pricing ranges from sixty to over four thousand dollars per user per year depending on modules selected.

FOUNDATION Software's T and M Billing Module captures every billable expense, automates markups, and produces client-ready invoices for time-and-material contracts. FOUNDATION acquired Vendrix in June 2025, adding corporate cards, expense management, and AP automation to the platform. Pricing is custom-quoted.

SmartBarrel combines AI biometric facial recognition for time tracking with PPE compliance monitoring at clock-in, giving specialty subs a dual-purpose tool. Pricing is custom-quoted.

Rhumbix, now acquired by Autodesk with the deal closing March thirty-first, twenty twenty-six, was a mobile-first time and materials tracking platform that automates T and M ticket generation directly from field time cards and captures unplanned work in real time. Integration into Autodesk Construction Cloud and Autodesk Forma is underway, with no changes to products or customer support in the near term. Pricing is custom-quoted.

Siteline is the billing and pay application platform built exclusively for commercial specialty subcontractors. It automates AIA pay app preparation, lien waiver collection, and vendor compliance tracking. Customers bill six times faster and reduce invoice aging by thirty percent on average. Pricing is custom-quoted.

Adaptive is an AI-native construction accounting platform that deploys AI agents to automate manual accounting work and deliver real-time financial control. Pricing starts at five hundred seventy-five dollars per month for businesses with up to five million dollars in annual revenue.

SubBase is an AI-powered materials procurement platform for specialty subcontractors that uses AI to automatically compare purchase orders, invoices, and delivery tickets for three-way reconciliation, completing the process in an average of twenty-one seconds compared to three to five minutes of manual work. Since the feature launched, SubBase has processed more than four thousand invoices covering over three and a half million dollars in purchases. Pricing is custom-quoted.

Payra is a B2B accounts receivable automation platform built for construction contractors running on legacy ERP systems, raising fifteen million dollars from Edison Partners in February 2026. It integrates directly with Trimble Viewpoint, FOUNDATION, Sage, and others to automate cash application and payment reconciliation without replacing existing systems. Customers have documented a twenty percent reduction in days sales outstanding and a seventy-five percent drop in past-due invoices. Pricing is transaction-based.

Intuit launched its Enterprise Suite Construction Edition on February eleventh, twenty twenty-six, delivering a purpose-built AI-powered ERP for mid-market contractors. It includes project phases with AI-enabled cost group tracking, AIA-style invoicing, negative change orders, proposal tools with e-signatures, and an AI Project Management Agent for cash flow and profitability monitoring. It is currently in open beta at no additional cost for existing Intuit Enterprise Suite customers, and available as a paid add-on for QuickBooks Online Advanced.

Trimble Pay, formerly Flashtract and fully integrated into Trimble Construction One following Trimble's 2024 acquisition, streamlines pay application submission, lien waiver collection and storage, certificate of insurance tracking, and compliance document management for both GCs and subcontractors. It is free for subcontractors to submit billing documents when invited by a primary contractor.

Trayd is an all-in-one back-office platform for specialty trade contractors covering payroll, HR, compliance, and labor cost tracking. It handles complex wage calculations including multi-state tax obligations, prevailing wage and Davis-Bacon compliance, union deductions by trade type, and project scope distinctions between state, federal, and private work. Trayd raised ten million dollars in a Series A in March 2026 and delivers a thirty-one times productivity gain on payroll, reducing the weekly cycle from fourteen hours to twenty-seven minutes.

inBuild is an AI-powered invoice processing platform built specifically for contractors running Procore and QuickBooks. It automatically centralizes invoices arriving by email, uses AI to match them to the correct project, vendor, and commitment in Procore, and syncs approved invoices back to QuickBooks without manual entry. Pricing starts at two hundred ninety-nine dollars per month.

Field Materials is an AI procurement platform for specialty subcontractors and self-performing general contractors that automates materials purchasing from request for quotes through purchase orders, invoices, and delivery receipt matching. Its Pricing Intelligence module launched in February 2026 shows real-time material price fluctuations and predicts when contractors can save by locking in prices with suppliers, particularly valuable amid tariff-driven cost volatility. The platform processes over seven hundred million dollars in material purchases annually across twelve construction trades and thirty US states. Total funding is approximately nineteen million dollars. Pricing is custom-quoted.

On change order and document management. Document Crunch is the AI contract review platform that reads construction contracts, specs, and insurance documents and surfaces risk clauses, change order triggers, and compliance obligations in plain language. Trimble announced its acquisition of Document Crunch on April 2, 2026, with the deal expected to close in the second quarter of 2026 and full integration planned into the Trimble Construction One ecosystem. The platform now serves more than four hundred customers processing three hundred fifty billion dollars in construction volume across ten thousand project kickoffs.

Procore's change order management with AI Assist lets project teams identify scope gaps, draft change order language, and track financial impacts in real time. Pricing is on annual construction volume.

Pelles AI is a generative AI platform that creates RFIs, submittal logs, and proposals in your company's existing format, giving smaller teams the output speed of a much larger document production operation. Pricing is custom-quoted.

Clearstory has had a major 2026 with significant new deployments and product updates. Suffolk Construction named Clearstory the standard for change order management across its national portfolio in March 2026, joining Turner Construction which has deployed the platform on more than three hundred fifty projects. New AI features include AI Reason Categories, which automatically classifies change orders into root cause categories for change order intelligence; Bulk AI Import, which brings entire COR backlogs into the system with AI-driven field mapping; a new Sage Intacct integration; and Procore Contract Mapping for automatic contract data population. Specialty contractors get change orders approved up to seventy-five percent faster and cut processing time by seventy percent or more. The basic tier is free for all stakeholders.

Bluebeam Max launched globally on May nineteenth, twenty twenty-six, adding an AI intelligence layer on top of the construction document platform used by millions of AEC professionals. Smart Review scans construction documents for design issues, scope gaps, and discrepancies. Smart Overlay detects design changes across disciplines and drawing scales, critical for pricing change order impacts. VisualSearch uses AI for quantity takeoffs, show Revu an image to automatically identify and count all other instances across drawings. It integrates with Anthropic Claude via the Model Context Protocol and with Autodesk Revit through Connected Studio Sessions. Pricing starts at five hundred ninety dollars per user per year introductory through 2027 renewal.

Structured AI is an AI drawing QA/QC and compliance checking platform that scans every engineering drawing sheet against building codes, firm standards, fire, accessibility, and coordination requirements simultaneously. Multiple independent AI agents review each drawing, returning exact page location, what is wrong, and how to fix it, across IBC, ASHRAE 90.1, NEC, ADA, and NFPA 13 requirements. Named to BuiltWorlds forty AI-Driven AEC Solutions to Watch in 2026. Pricing is by contact.

Buildcheck is an AI-powered design review platform founded at Stanford University that scans construction drawings across all major disciplines, including architectural, structural, civil, mechanical, electrical, and plumbing, to detect errors, omissions, and coordination conflicts before work begins. The platform delivers ten to thirty-five times return on investment by preventing costly field issues during pre-construction. Buildcheck raised five point nine million dollars in seed funding in December 2025 led by Uncork Capital with angels from OpenAI, CBRE, Zillow, and Opendoor. More than fifty construction organizations are using the platform, including AvalonBay Communities and Novo Construction. Pricing is not publicly listed.

Now for proposal generation and pre-bid analysis. BidIntell's pre-bid scoring anchors the proposal phase, helping specialty subs invest estimating hours only on the invitations that fit their win profile before a single takeoff is started. Pricing starts at forty-nine dollars per user per month.

Flowcase is an AI proposal management platform that matches company experience and employee qualifications to RFP requirements and auto-populates bid templates. Especially strong for design-assist and qualifications-based work. Pricing is custom-quoted.

ContraVault takes a team from opportunity qualification to a submission-ready proposal using AI to assemble, draft, and review content. Built for GC and specialty sub teams competing for complex awards. Average bid preparation time has dropped from thirty-four hours to fourteen hours per project for contractors using AI-powered estimating, and those contractors reported nineteen percent fewer cost overruns and twenty-two percent fewer change orders traced to estimating errors.

ConCntric is an AI preconstruction platform that raised ten million dollars in a Series A in October 2025. It unifies estimating, planning, and procurement from concept to GMP, and its Amplify agentic AI solution enables preconstruction teams to plan more thoroughly and respond to client needs with a fraction of the manual effort. Customers include Consigli, Big-D Construction, and Berglund Construction.

Provision is a Y Combinator-backed preconstruction risk reduction platform with eight and a half million dollars in total funding. Its Scope Agent generates a complete scope-of-work package from drawings, specs, and addenda in under sixty minutes, replacing thirty to forty hours of manual review per bid, with ninety-nine point five percent accuracy on pre-built risk checklists. Pricing is custom-quoted.

MeltPlan raised fourteen million dollars in total funding including a ten million dollar seed round led by Bessemer Venture Partners announced in February 2026. Melt Bid, its AI bid leveling product, reads subcontractor PDF proposals, maps them against the project scope and RFP, flags exclusions and missing line items, and outputs a normalized comparison matrix showing the true cost basis of each bid. AI bid leveling adoption among top four hundred GCs grew three hundred forty percent from 2024 to 2026.

LeanCon is an AI preconstruction planning platform that generates detailed projections covering scope definition, cost estimation, logistics planning, and construction sequencing in approximately seven minutes, replacing a process that traditionally takes months and costs upward of two million dollars. Founded by Yale School of Management alumni, LeanCon raised six million dollars in a seed round in 2026 led by Ibex Investors. The platform has over six hundred fifty million dollars in live project deployments across New York. Pricing is by contact.

On project scheduling with AI. ALICE Technologies formalized a partnership with McKinsey and Company in April 2026 to bring generative scheduling to capital project clients across infrastructure, data centers, and energy, introducing the technology to more than thirty-five clients with schedule accelerations of up to twenty percent. A McKinsey data center client reduced its entire construction schedule by forty percent. The platform's Schedule Insights Agent allows teams to interact with their schedule using natural language. Earlier documented results include a seventeen percent reduction in project duration, fourteen percent labor cost savings, and twelve percent equipment cost savings. Pricing is by custom quote.

Phoenix Project Manager is built for specialty contractors who need resource-loaded critical path schedules without the complexity of Primavera P six. Its labor histogram view lets electrical, mechanical, and plumbing contractors see workforce demand across their entire project portfolio. Starting at eighteen hundred dollars per year.

Oracle Primavera P six remains the industry standard on large complex projects at roughly twenty-one hundred to twenty-five hundred dollars per user per year, and its AI capabilities are being extended through ALICE and third-party integrations.

nPlan announced a partnership with the UK highways sector in February 2026, further extending its AI scheduling capabilities trained on seven hundred fifty thousand historical schedules representing over two trillion dollars of construction spend. The company raised sixteen million dollars in a Series B in October 2025, bringing total funding to thirty-six and a half million dollars. Anglian Water signed on to use nPlan to assure and manage up to fourteen hundred projects across its AMP8 investment window from 2025 to 2030, with live reporting to three hundred or more stakeholders. Pricing is by custom quote.

SmartPM is an AI-driven automated project controls and schedule analytics platform that integrates with existing CPM scheduling software to flag delays and critical path risks in real time. Total funding is thirteen and a half million dollars. Pricing starts at four hundred dollars per project per month.

Outbuild launched Project Intelligence in April 2026, adding a mobile field app for superintendents and foremen, an AI Assistant for natural language schedule queries, and an executive dashboard for portfolio-wide visibility. Trusted by four hundred or more customers managing over fifty billion dollars in active projects. Pricing scales based on annual construction volume.

Planera is an AI-powered CPM scheduling platform with unlimited-user collaboration and project-based pricing. It raised twenty-six and a half million dollars in total funding and has experienced six hundred percent growth in six months. Pricing is project-based. Integrates with Oracle Primavera P six.

Finally, safety and compliance monitoring. Intenseye uses existing camera infrastructure to detect more than fifty categories of unsafe acts and conditions in real time. Named a World Economic Forum Global Innovator in February 2026. Integrated NVIDIA Cosmos Reason 2 in March 2026 to advance vision AI capabilities and launched Intenseye Chief, an EHS-trained AI assistant that turns safety data into actionable insights. Total funding is approximately ninety-four million dollars. Pricing by custom quote based on camera count.

Voxel AI offers AI-powered safety monitoring at an estimated thirty to fifty percent lower cost than comparable enterprise platforms. Total funding now at eighty million dollars including a forty-four million dollar Series B. In April 2026, Voxel launched Risk Insights, an AI-powered daily safety snapshot that continuously analyzes facility video data and surfaces the most critical risks and emerging safety trends as concise, actionable summaries delivered to site managers by email and SMS. Pricing is structured around camera count.

Oracle launched its Construction and Engineering Advisor for Safety in March 2026, adding predictive analytics that forecast where safety incidents are likely before they happen. Built for large GC and owner teams running complex projects.

Pillar Technologies uses connected environmental sensors alongside visual monitoring to deliver real-time construction site risk management, with alerts covering temperature, moisture, atmospheric conditions, and visual hazard detection. Pricing is custom-quoted.

DroneDeploy Safety AI analyzes drone imagery and site camera data against OSHA standards to detect construction hazards including missing guardrails, exposed edges, and PPE violations. Beta customers reduced the occurrence of unsafe conditions by up to eighty-nine percent within three weeks. Pricing for the individual plan starts at three hundred forty-nine dollars per month billed annually.

viAct transforms existing site cameras into AI safety monitors using more than two hundred specialized detection modules. Pricing starts at two hundred dollars per camera per module per month, with a fourteen-day free trial.

Fyld raised forty-one million dollars in a Series B round in February 2026 and reported eighty-two percent year-over-year growth in 2025. Contractors report up to forty-eight percent reduction in serious workplace incidents. Customers include Kiewit, Quanta Services, and Emery Sapp and Sons. Pricing is by custom quote.

Safety Evolution's SE-AI platform replaces more than twenty hours of weekly administrative safety work with three AI agents covering compliance monitoring, workforce training management, and safety intelligence. It includes built-in support for COR and SECOR compliance and ISNetworld integration. Pricing is tiered per user.

Kwant dot AI is an AI-powered workforce management and safety platform that combines ATEX-certified smart wearables, IoT sensors, and AI analytics to automate safety orientation, track certifications, and detect incidents in real time. Pricing starts at approximately fifty thousand dollars for a single-site rollout including wearables and sensors, with custom pricing for portfolio deployments. It is built for both general contractors and specialty subcontractors.

Hakimo AI is an AI-powered physical security and safety monitoring platform for construction jobsites. Skanska has deployed Hakimo AI to proactively monitor major infrastructure projects, using AI-driven camera surveillance to prevent unauthorized access, detect safety conditions, and flag incidents in real time. Hakimo raised ten and a half million dollars in a Series A in 2025, bringing total funding to twenty and a half million dollars. Customers have documented a ninety percent drop in incidents and security cost reductions of up to two-thirds compared to traditional guard services. Pricing is by custom quote based on camera count.

Sensera Systems raised twenty-seven million dollars in a Series B in February 2026 and offers an AI-powered jobsite intelligence platform combining solar-powered, cellular-connected cameras with AI through its SiteCloud software. Cameras deploy in fifteen minutes with no on-site power or internet required, and SiteCloud Insights delivers real-time AI summaries covering site conditions, safety performance, equipment usage, and security. AI-powered SiteCloud Search identifies more than twenty visual event categories for instant footage retrieval without reviewing hours of video. Pricing is custom-quoted based on camera count.

Now for market context. On what owners and large GCs are expecting: major general contractors are increasingly requiring specialty subcontractors to actively manage submittals, RFIs, and daily documentation inside Procore or Autodesk Build as a condition of project participation. BIM coordination and model-based submittals are expected on commercial projects above ten million dollars. Digital daily reports with photo documentation and real-time cost tracking are moving from nice-to-have to contractual obligation. Subs who cannot interface digitally with GC platforms are being filtered from bid lists before the first conversation happens. AI adoption in construction has now doubled year over year, with thirty-eight percent of contractors reporting measurable business impact from AI, up from seventeen percent just one year ago, and ninety-four percent of those using AI plan to increase their usage in 2026.

On realistic adoption cost for a specialty sub with five to twenty project managers: a functional AI stack covering estimating, field documentation, submittal management, and billing automation typically runs between fifteen thousand and eighty thousand dollars per year in software costs. Training represents an additional fifteen to twenty percent of the total investment and is the factor most consistently underestimated. Implementation typically takes sixty to ninety days before teams reach operational efficiency. Most teams recover their AI investment within three to six months.

On documented ROI: a 2025 Bluebeam industry report found that sixty-eight percent of AI early adopters in construction have saved at least fifty thousand dollars, and forty-six percent have recovered between five hundred and one thousand hours of labor. The average return across the industry is three and a half times the investment, with top performers reporting ten times ROI. Bid preparation time is down by as much as sixty percent, and contractors using AI tools can respond to two to three times more RFQs without adding headcount. Clark Construction documented three point two million dollars in project savings through AI-assisted risk management. Firms using AI scheduling report twenty to thirty percent fewer project delays. Payra customers using AI accounts receivable automation document a twenty percent reduction in days sales outstanding and a seventy-five percent drop in past-due invoices. Trayd customers report a thirty-one times productivity gain on payroll processing, reducing the weekly payroll cycle from fourteen hours to twenty-seven minutes. The AI in construction market is valued at approximately eleven point one billion dollars in 2025 and is forecast to reach twenty-four point three billion dollars by 2030, advancing at a sixteen point nine percent CAGR. The message for specialty subs is clear: the tools are commercially available today, the ROI is documented and growing, and the window to adopt before digital capability becomes a competitive prerequisite is closing fast.
