Acquired Beam Finance Inc. in March 2026, adding AI estimating, payments, and invoicing to the platform. AI Note and Quick Caption features let field crews talk while taking photos with AI formatting output into structured notes and reports.
tools.pricing: From $99/month (3-user minimum); Enterprise custom pricing
tools.results: $2B valuation as of August 2025. 200,000+ contractor users. AI features in Premium and Elite tiers.
tools.integrations: Procore, Buildertrend, JobNimbus, QuickBooks, Xero, and 20+ other platforms
tools.spoken: CompanyCam is a photo-first field documentation platform that achieved unicorn status after closing a Series C funding round in August 2025. Its AI Walkthrough Note feature lets a superintendent walk the site, speak observations, and snap photos, and AI automatically assembles a structured daily report. CompanyCam acquired Beam Finance in March 2026 to add AI estimating, payments, and invoicing to the platform. Plans start at ninety-nine dollars per month with a three-user minimum.

tools.name: eSUB
tools.description: Mobile-first field management platform built exclusively for commercial specialty subcontractors. Added meaningful AI capabilities in 2026 including automated daily report drafting from field supervisor voice notes, predictive labor cost analysis flagging tasks trending over budget, and intelligent RFI drafting that suggests relevant specification sections.
tools.pricing: Custom quote; positioned as more affordable than Procore for sub-only teams
tools.results: AI daily report drafting reduced foreman paperwork from 45 minutes to 10 minutes per day. 95% of customers fully operational within 90 days.
tools.integrations: Sage 300 (integration released), major accounting platforms, drawing and design platforms
tools.spoken: eSUB is the subcontractor-exclusive field management platform that has added meaningful AI capabilities in 2026, including automated daily report drafting from field supervisor voice notes, predictive labor cost analysis that flags tasks trending over budget, and intelligent RFI drafting that suggests relevant specification sections. A mechanical sub using the AI daily report drafting feature reduced foreman paperwork from forty-five minutes to ten minutes per day. Pricing is by custom quote.

tools.name: Buildots
tools.description: AI construction progress tracking and workforce intelligence platform using 360-degree cameras worn on hardhats to automatically compare actual progress against the project schedule. Raised $45M Series D (May 2025) led by Qumra Capital; total raised $166M. Acquired Genda (workforce and safety management platform) in October 2025. In April 2026 launched under the Construction Intelligence positioning, unifying fragmented site data into a living operational model. 2026: Released Integrated Tracking feature enabling unified tracking of AI-automated and manually-recorded tasks.
tools.pricing: Custom quote based on project size
tools.results: Projects report up to 50% reduction in delays (2-3 months saved). Customers include DPR, Hensel Phelps, Clark Construction, and JE Dunn (via Genda).
tools.integrations: Oracle Primavera P6, Asta Power Project, Microsoft Project, Procore
tools.spoken: Buildots has added major new construction firms to its customer roster in 2026, now including STO Building Group, Mortenson, Ledcor, and Pomerleau alongside existing clients Turner Construction, JE Dunn, Digital Realty, and Intel. The platform uses helmet-mounted cameras and computer vision to capture real-time site progress against the schedule. Total funding reached one hundred sixty-six million dollars on a forty-five million dollar Series D. Pricing is custom-quoted by project size.

tools.name: Raken
tools.description: All-in-one field management platform for construction subcontractors and GCs covering daily reporting, time tracking, scheduling, safety management, workforce management, and project management. Sverica Capital Management acquired a majority stake in September 2025. New AI features include AI Photo ID for biometric clock-in and frictionless RFI creation. Added QuickBooks Desktop and CMiC integrations in 2025. 4,500+ construction firms and 70,000+ users.
tools.pricing: $15/user/month (Lite), $25/user/month (Pro); annual billing saves ~15%; 15-day free trial
tools.results: 4,500+ construction firms and 70,000+ users. Sverica Capital investment in September 2025 signals significant growth push.
tools.integrations: Procore, Autodesk Construction Cloud, QuickBooks, major payroll platforms
tools.spoken: Raken received a majority stake investment from Sverica Capital in September 2025, giving the field reporting platform new private equity backing to accelerate product development and market expansion. Raken now serves more than forty-five hundred construction firms and seventy thousand users with daily reports, time tracking, toolbox talks, and production tracking. Pricing starts at fifteen dollars per user per month for the Lite plan and twenty-five dollars per user per month for Pro.

tools.name: BuildOps
tools.description: Unified commercial contractor management platform for specialty trades covering both service operations and construction project management. Raised $127M Series C (March 2025) achieving unicorn status at $1B valuation; total funding $225.8M. Revenue reached $97.4M in 2025, up from $51.9M in 2024. In May 2026 launched BuildOps Projects: an AI-native construction project management system for specialty trades covering multi-month work from kickoff to closeout, including RFIs, submittals, change orders, retainage tracking, and subcontract management. OpsAI surfaces margin slips in real time and auto-generates invoices from technician field notes.
tools.pricing: Custom quote
tools.results: 1,500+ specialty contractors on platform. Revenue grew 88% year-over-year in 2025. BuildOps Projects delivers the first AI-native system built specifically for specialty trades to run multi-month construction work.
tools.integrations: QuickBooks, Sage, ServiceTitan workflow compatibility; Projects module integrates billing, change orders, and field data in one system
tools.spoken: BuildOps launched its Projects module in May 2026, giving specialty contractors an AI-native system to run multi-month construction work from kickoff to closeout alongside their service operations. Powered by OpsAI for scheduling, document management, field coordination, and daily reporting. BuildOps raised one hundred twenty-seven million dollars in a Series C round, reaching a one billion dollar valuation. Pricing is by custom quote.

tools.name: InspectMind AI
tools.description: Y Combinator-backed AI platform for construction field inspections and plan checking. Field Reports product captures site photos and multi-language voice notes on mobile, works offline, and generates structured daily reports, punch lists, and site walkthroughs using AI.
tools.pricing: $100/user/month (Field Reports); plan checking from $100/check or ~$1/drawing sheet at scale
tools.results: Generates field reports in minutes vs. hours; multi-language support including Spanish and Chinese to English; offline functionality for remote sites.
tools.integrations: Procore, Autodesk Construction Cloud
tools.spoken: InspectMind AI is a Y Combinator-backed platform that generates field reports, punch lists, safety inspections, and site walkthroughs in minutes. Its Field Reports product captures photos and voice notes on mobile, works offline, and produces structured reports at one hundred dollars per user per month. Plan checking starts at one hundred dollars per review. It integrates with Procore and Autodesk Construction Cloud.

tools.name: iFieldSmart
tools.description: Construction management platform with reality capture and AI-powered field documentation. Its Lens360 feature converts a single 360-degree site walkthrough into a complete digital twin with actionable insights, documentation, and progress tracking. Includes RFI and submittal tracking, task scheduling, and risk management.
tools.pricing: Starting at $14.99/user/month; free trial available; custom pricing for enterprise
tools.results: Single 360 walkthrough generates full site documentation and digital twin intelligence; reduces site visit time and documentation overhead.
tools.integrations: Procore, Autodesk Construction Cloud, Airtasks, Assemble
tools.spoken: iFieldSmart from iFieldSmart Technologies is a construction management platform with reality capture and AI-powered field documentation. Its Lens three-sixty feature turns a single site walkthrough into a complete digital twin. Pricing starts at fourteen dollars and ninety-nine cents per user per month.

tools.name: Kahua
tools.description: Enterprise construction project management platform for large GCs, program managers, and public sector owners. In May 2026 launched Noa, an embedded AI assistant that automates workflows, improves cost and reporting visibility, and manages capital programs through natural language interaction. Kahua Enterprise AI is a FedRAMP-authorized suite. Added IBM Maximo integration (November 2025) bridging construction project delivery with asset lifecycle management. US Army Corps of Engineers and Infrastructure Ontario are active Kahua customers.
tools.pricing: Custom quote; implementation costs typically $5,000-$50,000+ depending on scale
tools.results: Named Top Construction Technology Firm in Construction Executive's 2025 Top Tech report. US Army Corps of Engineers and Infrastructure Ontario deployments.
tools.integrations: Custom integrations with major accounting and project management platforms
tools.spoken: Kahua is a flexible construction management platform with built-in AI through its Noa assistant, which automates repetitive project workflows, uncovers insights from project data, and lets teams build custom apps in minutes. Pricing is custom-quoted with implementation costs typically ranging from five thousand to fifty thousand dollars or more.

tools.name: OpenSpace
tools.description: AI-powered 360-degree site capture and visual intelligence platform. Acquired Disperse (AI construction progress tracking company) in October 2025. In February 2026 launched OpenSpace Field in general availability: an image-based, field-first task and issue management system extending OpenSpace from a system of visual record into a system of visual action. AI Autolocation automatically tags where an issue occurred on site. AI Voice Notes enable hands-free capture that auto-fills form fields from speech. Total funding $199M.
tools.pricing: Annual Construction Volume (ACV)-based; Core and Enterprise tiers; custom quote
tools.results: OpenSpace Field GA February 2026. Suffolk Construction: 86% speed improvement in documenting issues. Vinci Construction UK: 6,000+ hours saved annually.
tools.integrations: Procore (bidirectional), Autodesk Construction Cloud (bidirectional)
tools.spoken: OpenSpace is a Visual Intelligence Platform that uses three-sixty cameras to automatically map site photos to floor plans and BIM models. It launched OpenSpace Field in general availability in February 2026, an image-based field task and issue management solution that connects captured site reality directly to execution workflows. Suffolk Construction documented an eighty-six percent speed improvement in documenting issues using the platform's AI Autolocation and AI Voice Notes. Vinci Construction UK saved six thousand or more hours annually on documentation. Pricing is based on annual construction volume.

tools.name: Novade
tools.description: AI-powered construction field management platform used by 100,000+ users in 25+ countries. Key AI features include speech-to-forms, scan-to-form, AI-powered daily report generation, and a predictive safety monitoring engine with up to 88% accuracy for hazard risk scoring. Platform covers quality checklists, safety inspections, daily reports, punch lists, and project management workflows. $21.3M total funding.
tools.pricing: Free plan (up to 5 users, 5 projects); Standard and Premium plans with custom pricing; Enterprise plan for large deployments
tools.results: 100,000+ users across 25+ countries. AI predictive safety monitoring achieves up to 88% accuracy. Speech-to-forms and scan-to-form eliminate manual field data entry.
tools.integrations: Procore, Autodesk Construction Cloud, Oracle; IoT system API connections
tools.spoken: Novade Solutions is an AI-powered field management platform used by more than one hundred thousand users in twenty-five or more countries. Key AI features include speech-to-forms that convert field voice observations into structured mobile data, scan-to-form that photographs paper forms and converts them into digital templates, AI daily report generation, and a predictive safety monitoring engine trained on real-world incident data that achieves up to eighty-eight percent accuracy in hazard risk scoring. A free plan is available for up to five users and five projects.

tools.name: Benetics AI
tools.description: Voice-activated AI platform for construction field documentation including daily reporting, punch lists, and RFI creation, with real-time translation across 30+ languages. Expanded into US and Canada markets in 2025, with active deployments in Michigan. December 2025: expanded REST API with Make.com and Zapier connectors. January 2026: launched automatic detection of construction plans arriving in email. Now serves 250+ construction firms across Europe and North America.
tools.pricing: Tiered per-user plans (Solo, Team, Enterprise); free trial available; custom Enterprise pricing for 200+ employee firms
tools.results: 250+ construction firms across Europe and North America. Expanding in US/Canada market with Michigan deployments.
tools.integrations: Standalone mobile app (iOS and Android); voice-to-structured report workflow
tools.spoken: Benetics AI is an AI voice assistant for construction field documentation. Field crews speak naturally into the mobile app to capture site conditions, deliveries, safety observations, and progress updates, and AI converts voice to professional structured reports in seconds without stopping work. Two hundred fifty or more companies are using the platform across the US and Canada with users reporting up to seventy to eighty percent reduction in admin time. Available on iOS and Android.

tools.name: BuilderHelp
tools.description: AI-driven construction management platform targeting $5M-$65M general contractors, launched June 1, 2026. Features predictive AI scheduling with proactive timeline adjustments before delays occur, centralized communication across subcontractors, owners, and project teams. Developed by construction industry professionals. Based in Auburn, Alabama. Over 50 GCs on the waitlist at launch.
tools.pricing: Not publicly listed; contact company directly
tools.results: Launched June 1, 2026 with 50+ GC waitlist. Built by industry professionals targeting real-world jobsite communication and administrative challenges.
tools.integrations: Not yet published; platform launched June 2026
tools.spoken: BuilderHelp from BuilderHelp launched its AI construction management platform on June first, twenty twenty-six, targeting general contractors in the five million to sixty-five million dollar range. The platform was developed by industry professionals and features predictive AI scheduling, centralized communication across subs and project teams, task tracking with real-time progress visibility, invoice and budget monitoring, and document management for plans, specs, permits, and contracts. More than fifty general contractors were on the waitlist at launch. Pricing is available directly through the company.

tools.name: KYRO AI
tools.description: Unified field management platform for construction, vegetation management, and storm restoration contractors. Combines project management, crew communication, time tracking, payroll, and daily reporting in one mobile app. AI automatically translates voice notes from field crews into any language. OCR scans documents and auto-generates schedules from uploaded files. Available on iOS, Android, and web.
tools.pricing: $21/user/month (Basic, billed annually); $42/user/month (Pro, billed annually). Free trial available.
tools.built_for: Specialty subcontractors and small GCs, particularly utility, vegetation management, and storm restoration contractors
tools.results: No independently documented case studies published yet. Platform commercially available.
tools.integrations: Standalone platform; no confirmed Procore or Autodesk integrations.
tools.spoken: KYRO AI is a unified field management platform for construction, vegetation management, and storm restoration contractors. It combines project management, crew communication, time tracking, payroll, and daily reporting with AI voice translation across thirty or more languages and OCR document scanning. Pricing starts at twenty-one dollars per user per month billed annually.

tools.name: Klutch AI
tools.description: Multi-agent AI construction management platform offering specialized agents across the project lifecycle: Archie AI for zoning and permit review (up to 50% faster permit cycles), Bob AI for capturing punch items, safety forms, and daily jobsite updates via SMS, WhatsApp, and Telegram without requiring a portal login, Petra AI for takeoffs, estimates, bid leveling, and vendor scoring, and Hailey AI for warranty ticket management linked to vendor performance. Raised $8M seed round (June 2025) led by Bain Capital Ventures and Bling Capital.
tools.pricing: Not publicly listed; contact sales
tools.results: Saves 10+ hours/week per project manager. Petra AI boosts PM capacity by 35%. Covers pre-construction through project closeout in a single agent-driven platform.
tools.integrations: Procore, Autodesk Construction Cloud (planned)
tools.spoken: Klutch AI is a multi-agent AI construction management platform offering specialized agents across the project lifecycle, including Archie AI for permit review, Bob AI for field documentation via SMS and WhatsApp without a portal login, Petra AI for estimating and bid leveling, and Hailey AI for warranty ticket management. The platform raised eight million dollars in a seed round led by Bain Capital Ventures. Pricing is by contact.

tools.name: Opusense AI
tools.description: Y Combinator-backed (X25 batch) AI-powered field inspection report automation. Field inspectors walk a site, speak observations aloud, and snap photos; AI generates a detailed, properly formatted inspection report in real time without interrupting fieldwork. Available on iOS and Android with offline capability.
tools.pricing: Not publicly listed; contact sales
tools.results: Claims up to 80% reduction in inspection report production time. Y Combinator X25 batch backing. Available on iOS and Android with offline mode.
tools.integrations: iOS and Android mobile; cloud report storage and sharing, Fieldwire (observations convert to tasks in one click)
tools.spoken: Opusense AI is a Y Combinator-backed field inspection report automation platform. Field inspectors walk a site, speak observations aloud, and snap photos while AI generates a detailed inspection report in real time on iOS and Android. Claims up to eighty percent reduction in inspection report production time.

tools.name: Hardline
tools.description: Voice-first construction documentation tool that captures jobsite phone calls and verbal conversations in English and Spanish, transcribes them in real time, and uses AI to extract and structure action items into daily logs, RFIs, punch lists, change orders, tasks, and meeting notes automatically from the phone call itself. Suffolk Technologies BOOST Accelerator alumni. Raised $2M pre-seed led by Mucker Capital. Active across 10 states and 2 countries.
tools.pricing: Flexible early-access pricing by team size; contact sales
tools.results: Saves superintendents up to 3 hours/day in documentation time. Active across 10 states and 2 countries.
tools.integrations: Procore, Autodesk Forma, Hilti Fieldwire
tools.spoken: Hardline is a voice-first construction documentation tool that captures jobsite phone calls in English and Spanish, transcribes them in real time, and uses AI to extract and structure action items into daily logs, RFIs, punch lists, and change orders automatically from the conversation itself. It raised two million dollars in a pre-seed round from Mucker Capital and is active across ten states and two countries.

tools.name: Track3D
tools.description: AI-powered Reality Intelligence platform that unifies 360 cameras, drones, laser scanners, and LiDAR data into a single platform (VisualTrack) and applies AI-driven construction progress tracking (ProgressTrack). Quantifies work installed by trade and activity, generates automated progress reports, and enables payment milestone verification from captured site data. Named to BuiltWorlds 40 AI-Driven AEC Solutions to Watch in 2026.
tools.pricing: Custom quote based on project scale and capture hardware
tools.results: Named BuiltWorlds 2026 Top 40 AI-Driven AEC Solutions. Unifies 360, drone, laser scan, and LiDAR data for contractors running complex projects with mixed documentation equipment.
tools.integrations: Autodesk Construction Cloud, Procore
tools.spoken: Track3D is an AI Reality Intelligence platform that ingests three-sixty cameras, drones, laser scanners, and LiDAR data into a single platform, applies AI-driven progress tracking, quantifies work installed by trade, and generates automated progress reports. Named to BuiltWorlds forty AI-Driven AEC Solutions to Watch in 2026. Pricing is custom-quoted.

tools.name: Doxel AI
tools.description: AI-powered construction progress tracking platform that uses 360-degree cameras and computer vision to compare actual work-in-place against BIM models in real time. Tracks installed quantities across all visible trades and 80+ stages of construction, generating color-coded cost-versus-schedule dashboards that surface slippage early without manual input. Enables trade contractors to compare actual install rates to benchmarks and improve resource allocation. Total funding $56.5M from Insight Partners and Andreessen Horowitz. Named Forbes Best Startup Employer in America 2026. Integrates with Procore, Autodesk Construction Cloud, and Oracle Primavera P6.
tools.pricing: Custom quote based on project size and scope
tools.results: DPR Construction is among notable customers. Color-coded BIM-vs-reality dashboard gives GCs and subs early warning of schedule slippage. 114 employees as of early 2026 with active US and European project deployments.
tools.integrations: Procore, Autodesk Construction Cloud, Oracle Primavera P6, Touchplan, Allucent
tools.spoken: Doxel AI uses three-sixty cameras and computer vision to compare actual construction progress against BIM models in real time, automatically tracking work installed across all visible trades and more than eighty stages of construction. The platform generates color-coded cost versus schedule reports that give project teams early warning of slippage before it compounds. DPR Construction is among its notable customers. Doxel has raised fifty-six point five million dollars backed by Insight Partners and Andreessen Horowitz. It integrates with Procore, Autodesk Construction Cloud, and Oracle Primavera P six. Pricing is custom-quoted.

tools.name: HCSS HeavyJob
tools.description: AI-enhanced field operations and cost management platform for heavy civil and specialty contractors. 2026 Winter Release added HCSS Copilot AI: summarizes the Daily Digest, helps users navigate the platform, and answers questions using company knowledge base and job data. 2026 Spring Release added an AI-powered Summarize tool for audit logs, direct HeavyBid-to-HeavyJob estimate handoff, and Docs and Plans integrated directly into the HeavyJob mobile app.
tools.pricing: $60-$4,000+/user/year depending on modules; custom enterprise pricing
tools.results: Used by thousands of heavy civil contractors across North America. HCSS Copilot AI and Spring 2026 AI audit summaries reduce manual data review time significantly.
tools.integrations: Major construction accounting systems
tools.spoken: HCSS HeavyJob is the go-to standard for heavy and civil specialty contractors doing time-and-material work. It pulls T and M billing data directly from field time cards and generates accurate invoices with minimal manual effort. The 2026 Spring Release added a new AI-powered audit log summarization tool and direct HeavyBid-to-HeavyJob estimate handoff. Pricing ranges from sixty to over four thousand dollars per user per year depending on modules selected.

tools.name: FOUNDATION T&M Billing Module
tools.description: T&M billing and construction accounting platform backed by Thoma Bravo private equity. Acquired Vendrix, Inc. in June 2025, adding corporate cards, expense management, AP automation, and bill pay to cover nearly every stage of a construction project's financial lifecycle. Core features include time-and-material billing, certified payroll, subcontract management, and job costing.
tools.pricing: Custom quote as part of FOUNDATION accounting suite
tools.results: Produces precise T&M invoices; deep integration with construction accounting eliminates duplicate data entry.
tools.integrations: Sage, QuickBooks, Procore (via SubBase partner integration), Autodesk, Viewpoint; Vendrix adds AP automation and corporate card management
tools.spoken: FOUNDATION Software's T and M Billing Module captures every billable expense, automates markups, and produces client-ready invoices for time-and-material contracts. FOUNDATION acquired Vendrix in June 2025, adding corporate cards, expense management, and AP automation to the platform. Pricing is custom-quoted.

tools.name: SmartBarrel
tools.description: AI biometric workforce management system combining facial recognition time tracking with PPE compliance monitoring at clock-in, validating each worker against a saved facial profile even in jobsite conditions.
tools.pricing: Custom quote based on site count, worker count, and hardware deployment
tools.results: Eliminates buddy-punching; provides accurate T&M labor documentation; adds PPE compliance monitoring layer to time tracking hardware.
tools.integrations: Custom integrations with payroll and project management systems
tools.spoken: SmartBarrel combines AI biometric facial recognition for time tracking with PPE compliance monitoring at clock-in, giving specialty subs a dual-purpose tool. Pricing is custom-quoted.

tools.name: Rhumbix
tools.description: Mobile-first workforce tracking and T&M billing platform for construction. Autodesk completed its acquisition of Rhumbix on March 31, 2026, bringing real-time jobsite labor and payroll data into its construction platform. Rhumbix continues to operate products and customer support without changes; integration into Autodesk Construction Cloud and Autodesk Forma is underway.
tools.pricing: Custom quote based on user count
tools.results: 50% reduction in T&M processing time; digital T&M tag processing reduced from 2-6 weeks to 1-2 days; cost overruns caught 2-3 weeks earlier. Acquisition closed March 31, 2026.
tools.integrations: Autodesk Construction Cloud (acquisition integration in progress); Procore; QuickBooks Online Advanced; ADP Workforce Now
tools.spoken: Rhumbix, now acquired by Autodesk with the deal closing March thirty-first, twenty twenty-six, was a mobile-first time and materials tracking platform that automates T and M ticket generation directly from field time cards and captures unplanned work in real time. Integration into Autodesk Construction Cloud and Autodesk Forma is underway, with no changes to products or customer support in the near term. Pricing is custom-quoted.

tools.name: Siteline
tools.description: Billing and payment management platform built exclusively for commercial subcontractors. Automates pay applications in any custom GC format, manages lien waivers with AI validation (automatically flags missing signatures, incorrect amounts, and mismatched details), and handles accounts receivable reporting, collections management, and cash flow forecasting. AI lien waiver validation is in active production for all customers.
tools.pricing: Custom quote based on billing volume and integrations
tools.results: Customers save an average of 7 days per week across billing workflows. Payments sped up by an average of 3 weeks. AI lien waiver validation in production.
tools.integrations: Viewpoint Vista (two-way real-time sync), Procore Pay, Textura, GCPay, Foundation Software
tools.spoken: Siteline is the billing and pay application platform built exclusively for commercial specialty subcontractors. It automates AIA pay app preparation, lien waiver collection, and vendor compliance tracking. Customers bill six times faster and reduce invoice aging by thirty percent on average. Pricing is custom-quoted.

tools.name: Adaptive
tools.description: AI-native construction accounting platform that continuously captures and codes every cost to the correct job and cost code in real time. Builds accurate cost-plus and fixed-price billings directly from approved job costs with backup documentation attached. Automates job costing, WIP reporting, billing approvals, payments, and financial reporting. Won Best of IBS 2026 Award. Raised $19M Series A (July 2024) led by Emergence Capital with Andreessen Horowitz; total raised $26M.
tools.pricing: Starts at $575/month for businesses under $5M in annual revenue; revenue-based tiers above that threshold
tools.results: Best of IBS 2026 Award winner (Business Solution Software category). No project-level case studies published yet.
tools.integrations: QuickBooks (real-time two-way sync); API integrations with construction management platforms
tools.spoken: Adaptive is an AI-native construction accounting platform that deploys AI agents to automate manual accounting work and deliver real-time financial control. Pricing starts at five hundred seventy-five dollars per month for businesses with up to five million dollars in annual revenue.

tools.name: SubBase
tools.description: AI-powered materials procurement and invoice reconciliation platform for specialty subcontractors and self-performing GCs. Uses AI to automatically compare purchase orders, invoices, and delivery tickets for three-way reconciliation, completing the process in an average of 21 seconds vs. 3-5 minutes manually. AI reconciliation feature has processed 4,000+ invoices covering $3.5M+ in purchases since launch.
tools.pricing: Custom quote
tools.results: 21-second average AI reconciliation vs. 3-5 minutes manual. 4,000+ invoices processed since feature launch, covering $3.5M+ in purchases.
tools.integrations: Custom integrations with accounting and ERP systems
tools.spoken: SubBase is an AI-powered materials procurement platform for specialty subcontractors that uses AI to automatically compare purchase orders, invoices, and delivery tickets for three-way reconciliation, completing the process in an average of twenty-one seconds compared to three to five minutes of manual work. Since the feature launched, SubBase has processed more than four thousand invoices covering over three and a half million dollars in purchases. Pricing is custom-quoted.

tools.name: Payra
tools.description: AI-enabled accounts receivable automation platform that embeds into existing construction ERPs to enable ACH and card digital payment acceptance, automate cash application, and reconcile payments to invoices in real time. Raised $15M growth investment from Edison Partners (February 2026) — first outside capital after being previously bootstrapped.
tools.pricing: Not publicly listed; pricing structured around ERP integration and transaction volume.
tools.results: 20% reduction in Days Sales Outstanding, 75% decrease in past-due invoices, significant admin time savings (customer-reported). $15M Edison Partners investment validates commercial traction.
tools.integrations: Trimble Viewpoint, Foundation Software, Sage, NetSuite, and other construction ERPs
tools.spoken: Payra is a B2B accounts receivable automation platform built for construction contractors running on legacy ERP systems, raising fifteen million dollars from Edison Partners in February 2026. It integrates directly with Trimble Viewpoint, FOUNDATION, Sage, and others to automate cash application and payment reconciliation without replacing existing systems. Customers have documented a twenty percent reduction in days sales outstanding and a seventy-five percent drop in past-due invoices. Pricing is transaction-based.

tools.name: Intuit Enterprise Suite Construction Edition
tools.description: Purpose-built AI-powered ERP for mid-market contractors launched February 11, 2026. Includes project phases with AI-enabled cost group tracking, AIA-style invoicing with automated change order processing, negative change order support, proposal tools with e-signatures, and an AI Project Management Agent for cash flow and profitability monitoring. Currently in open beta at no additional cost for existing Intuit Enterprise Suite customers.
tools.pricing: Open beta at no charge for existing Intuit Enterprise Suite customers; paid add-on for QuickBooks Online Advanced (pricing TBD)
tools.results: AI-driven cost and change order tracking available in February 2026 open beta. Positions Intuit as AI-first accounting platform for construction.
tools.integrations: Intuit ecosystem including QuickBooks Online Advanced
tools.spoken: Intuit launched its Enterprise Suite Construction Edition on February eleventh, twenty twenty-six, delivering a purpose-built AI-powered ERP for mid-market contractors. It includes project phases with AI-enabled cost group tracking, AIA-style invoicing, negative change orders, proposal tools with e-signatures, and an AI Project Management Agent for cash flow and profitability monitoring. It is currently in open beta at no additional cost for existing Intuit Enterprise Suite customers, and available as a paid add-on for QuickBooks Online Advanced.

tools.name: Trimble Pay
tools.description: Pay application and lien waiver management platform (formerly Flashtract) fully integrated into Trimble Construction One following Trimble's 2024 acquisition. Streamlines pay application submission, lien waiver collection and storage, certificate of insurance tracking, and compliance document management for both GCs and subcontractors. Free for subcontractors to submit billing documents when invited by a primary contractor using the platform.
tools.pricing: Free for subs submitting docs; GC pricing included in Trimble Construction One
tools.results: Seamless integration into Trimble Construction One; free sub access eliminates adoption friction.
tools.integrations: Trimble Construction One ecosystem; Viewpoint and other Trimble integrations
tools.spoken: Trimble Pay, formerly Flashtract and fully integrated into Trimble Construction One following Trimble's 2024 acquisition, streamlines pay application submission, lien waiver collection and storage, certificate of insurance tracking, and compliance document management for both GCs and subcontractors. It is free for subcontractors to submit billing documents when invited by a primary contractor.

tools.name: Trayd
tools.description: All-in-one back-office operating system for specialty trade contractors covering payroll, HR, compliance, and labor cost tracking. Handles complex wage calculations including multi-state tax obligations, prevailing wage and Davis-Bacon compliance, union deductions by trade type, and project scope distinctions between state, federal, and private work. Raised $10M Series A (March 2026) led by White Star Capital with Y Combinator and Suffolk Technologies; total funding $17M. 600%+ YoY revenue growth.
tools.pricing: SaaS subscription priced per worker processed through payroll; contact sales for specifics
tools.results: Reduces weekly payroll processing from 14 hours to 27 minutes — a 31x productivity gain. 600%+ year-over-year revenue growth. $10M Series A led by White Star Capital (March 2026).
tools.integrations: Integrates with construction accounting and payroll systems; field mobile app for time tracking
tools.spoken: Trayd is an all-in-one back-office platform for specialty trade contractors covering payroll, HR, compliance, and labor cost tracking. It handles complex wage calculations including multi-state tax obligations, prevailing wage and Davis-Bacon compliance, union deductions by trade type, and project scope distinctions between state, federal, and private work. Trayd raised ten million dollars in a Series A in March 2026 and delivers a thirty-one times productivity gain on payroll, reducing the weekly cycle from fourteen hours to twenty-seven minutes.

tools.name: inBuild
tools.description: AI-powered invoice processing platform purpose-built for contractors running Procore and QuickBooks. Automatically centralizes invoices arriving by email and from the field into a unified intelligent inbox, uses AI to match them to the correct project, vendor, and commitment in Procore, and syncs approved invoices back to QuickBooks Online or QuickBooks Desktop without manual entry. Eliminates duplicate data entry between field, Procore, and accounting. Available on the Procore App Marketplace.

tools.results: Eliminates manual invoice entry between Procore and QuickBooks. Real-time budget and project finance updates in Procore on invoice approval. Available on Procore App Marketplace.
tools.integrations: Procore (bidirectional real-time sync), QuickBooks Online, QuickBooks Desktop
tools.spoken: inBuild is an AI-powered invoice processing platform built specifically for contractors running Procore and QuickBooks. It automatically centralizes invoices arriving by email, uses AI to match them to the correct project, vendor, and commitment in Procore, and syncs approved invoices back to QuickBooks without manual entry. Pricing starts at two hundred ninety-nine dollars per month.

tools.name: Field Materials
tools.description: AI procurement platform for specialty subcontractors and self-performing general contractors that automates materials purchasing from RFQ through purchase orders, invoices, and delivery receipt matching. Modules cover RFQ automation, purchase order creation, inventory management, prefab order management, AI three-way invoice matching, and receipt verification. Launched Pricing Intelligence module in February 2026 — a real-time dashboard showing price fluctuations and predicting optimal times to lock in prices with suppliers, particularly valuable amid tariff-driven material cost volatility. Processes $700M+ in material purchases annually across 12 construction trades, 30 US states, and 4 countries. Founded 2022. Total funding approximately $19M. SMACNA member benefit partner.
tools.pricing: Custom quote; no publicly listed tier pricing
tools.results: Saves 5-10% in material costs and removes 90% of processing time for material purchasing. $700M+ in annual material purchases processed. 3x revenue growth year-over-year. SMACNA partnership validates mechanical and sheet metal sub applicability.
tools.integrations: Major construction ERPs (Viewpoint, Foundation, Sage, Procore) for purchase order, invoice, and receipt sync
tools.spoken: Field Materials is an AI procurement platform for specialty subcontractors and self-performing general contractors that automates materials purchasing from request for quotes through purchase orders, invoices, and delivery receipt matching. Its Pricing Intelligence module launched in February 2026 shows real-time material price fluctuations and predicts when contractors can save by locking in prices with suppliers, particularly valuable amid tariff-driven cost volatility. The platform processes over seven hundred million dollars in material purchases annually across twelve construction trades and thirty US states. Total funding is approximately nineteen million dollars. Pricing is custom-quoted.

March 2026: Suffolk Construction named Clearstory the standard for change order management across its national portfolio; Turner Construction deployed across 350+ projects. market_context.owner_and_gc_expectations: Major general contractors are increasingly requiring specialty subcontractors to actively manage submittals, RFIs, and daily documentation inside Procore or Autodesk Build as a condition of project participation. BIM coordination and model-based submittals are expected on commercial projects above ten million dollars. Digital daily reports with photo documentation and real-time cost tracking are moving from nice-to-have to contractual obligation. Subs who cannot interface digitally with GC platforms are being filtered from bid lists before the first conversation happens. AI adoption in construction has now doubled year over year, with thirty-eight percent of contractors reporting measurable business impact from AI, up from seventeen percent just one year ago, and ninety-four percent of those using AI plan to increase their usage in 2026.
market_context.realistic_adoption_cost: On realistic adoption cost for a specialty sub with five to twenty project managers: a functional AI stack covering estimating, field documentation, submittal management, and billing automation typically runs between fifteen thousand and eighty thousand dollars per year in software costs. Training represents an additional fifteen to twenty percent of the total investment and is the factor most consistently underestimated. Implementation typically takes sixty to ninety days before teams reach operational efficiency. Most teams recover their AI investment within three to six months.
market_context.documented_roi: A 2025 Bluebeam industry report found that sixty-eight percent of AI early adopters in construction have saved at least fifty thousand dollars, and forty-six percent have recovered between five hundred and one thousand hours of labor. The average return across the industry is three and a half times the investment, with top performers reporting ten times ROI. Bid preparation time is down by as much as sixty percent, and contractors using AI tools can respond to two to three times more RFQs without adding headcount. Clark Construction documented three point two million dollars in project savings through AI-assisted risk management. Firms using AI scheduling report twenty to thirty percent fewer project delays. Payra customers using AI accounts receivable automation document a twenty percent reduction in days sales outstanding and a seventy-five percent drop in past-due invoices. Trayd customers report a thirty-one times productivity gain on payroll processing, reducing the weekly payroll cycle from fourteen hours to twenty-seven minutes. The AI in construction market is valued at approximately eleven point one billion dollars in 2025 and is forecast to reach twenty-four point three billion dollars by 2030, advancing at a sixteen point nine percent CAGR. The message for specialty subs is clear: the tools are commercially available today, the ROI is documented and growing, and the window to adopt before digital capability becomes a competitive prerequisite is closing fast.
